design_philosophy.core_loop: Descend through multi-level dungeon: fight enemies, solve puzzles, rescue kidnapped townspeople, acquire magic and items, progress to next floor
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Exploration vs. combat readiness—players must balance discovering new areas and townspeople with managing health and resources across a large, unmapped dungeon
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Knight hero rescuing an entire town from a dark conspiracy, wielding elemental magic and growing in power as you delve deeper into mystery
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Isometric perspective as a core mechanical pillar; emphasis on elemental magic combination system (up to 4 elements simultaneously) rather than traditional progression; dungeon-first narrative (story unfolds through exploration and NPC dialogue, not cutscenes)

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can manage difficulty through item inventory, magic selection via pause menu, and exploration pace; ability to revisit towns to purchase healing items and gear

progression.upgrade_system: Gear-based: items that increase offensive or defensive power acquired through dungeon exploration and shop purchases; magic system allows elemental combinations for buffs and attacks
progression.unlock_mechanism: Descent through dungeon floors; rescue of townspeople unlocks story progression and access to new towns within dungeon; acquiring different magic types expands combat and utility options
replayability_hooks: Player-filled map system encourages thorough exploration on replays
Multiple magic combinations create different playstyle approaches
Optional NPC dialogue and townspeople interactions for lore discovery

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through dungeon architecture and layout (graveyard entrance, wizard guild, goblin town)
NPC dialogue in hub towns (Green Row and in-dungeon towns)
Character discovery through rescue mechanics (kidnapped townspeople as story reveals)
Antagonist motivation rooted in personal rejection (Ragno Roke's revenge plot)

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters punctuate floor progression and story beats; designed to showcase magic system and combat variety
notable_bosses: Ragno Roke (wizard antagonist, sacrifices self to revive Ramiah)
Ramiah (demon, final boss sealed within dungeon)

faction_indicators: Wizard guild faction (living in dungeon, separate from Green Row)
Goblin town faction (inhabits lower dungeon levels)
Green Row monarchy (surface authority, initiates quest)
danger_signals: Wizard Ragno Roke's jilted revenge motive (rejected marriage proposal)
Ritualistic kidnapping of townspeople for demon resurrection
Sealed demon (Ramiah) at dungeon core suggests historical catastrophe
environmental_storytelling: Graveyard concealing dungeon entrance (death and underworld imagery)
Multi-level dungeon with distinct zones (farm, wizard guild, goblin settlement)
Castle architecture representing monarchy control vs. dungeon chaos
Progression from surface town through increasingly alien underground spaces
